channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: AI Conundrum: Why MCP Security Can't Be Patched Away
item.description: MCP introduces security risks into LLM environments that are architectural and not easily fixable, researcher says at RSAC 2026 Conference....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Navia discloses data breach impacting 2.7 million people
item.description: Navia Benefit Solutions, Inc. (Navia) is informing nearly 2.7 million individuals of a data breach that exposed their sensitive information to attackers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New ‘PolyShell’ flaw allows unauthenticated RCE on Magento e-stores
item.description: A newly disclosed vulnerability dubbed 'PolyShell' affects all Magento Open Source and Adobe Commerce stable version 2 installations, allowing unauthenticated code execution and account takeover. [......
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Speagle Malware Hijacks Cobra DocGuard to Steal Data via Compromised Servers
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malware dubbed Speagle that hijacks the functionality and infrastructure of a legitimate program called Cobra DocGuard. "Speagle is designed to surreptitio...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 54 EDR Killers Use BYOVD to Exploit 34 Signed Vulnerable Drivers and Disable Security
item.description: A new analysis of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers has revealed that 54 of them leverage a technique known as bring your own vulnerable driver (BYOVD) by abusing a total of 34 vulnerable ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Bitrefill blames North Korean Lazarus group for cyberattack
item.description: Crypto-powered gift card store Bitrefill says that the attack it suffered at the beginning of the month was likely perpetrated by North Korean hackers of the Bluenoroff group. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FBI seizes Handala data leak site after Stryker cyberattack
item.description: The FBI has seized two websites used by the Handala hacktivist group after the threat actors conducted a destructive cyberattack on medical technology giant Stryker that wiped approximately 80,000 dev...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Russian hackers exploit Zimbra flaw in Ukrainian govt attacks
item.description: Hackers part of APT28, a state-backed threat group linked to Russia's military intelligence service (GRU), are exploiting a Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS) vulnerability in attacks targeting Ukrainia...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: FortiGate RaaS, Citrix Exploits, MCP Abuse, LiveChat Phish & More
item.description: ThreatsDay Bulletin is back on The Hacker News, and this week feels off in a familiar way. Nothing loud, nothing breaking everything at once. Just a lot of small things that shouldn’t work anymore but...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 7 Ways to Prevent Privilege Escalation via Password Resets
item.description: Password resets are often weaker than login security, making them a prime target for privilege escalation. Specops Software explains how attackers abuse reset workflows and how to secure them. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Max severity Ubiquiti UniFi flaw may allow account takeover
item.description: Ubiquiti has patched two vulnerabilities in the UniFi Network Application, including a maximum-severity flaw that may allow attackers to take over user accounts. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New Perseus Android Banking Malware Monitors Notes Apps to Extract Sensitive Data
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a new Android malware family called Perseus that's being actively distributed in the wild with an aim to conduct device takeover (DTO) and financial fraud. Per...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA urges US orgs to secure Microsoft Intune systems after Stryker breach
item.description: CISA warned U.S. organizations to follow Microsoft guidance to strengthen the Intune endpoint management tool after a cyberattack exploited it to wipe medical technology giant Stryker's systems. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: How Ceros Gives Security Teams Visibility and Control in Claude Code
item.description: Security teams have spent years building identity and access controls for human users and service accounts. But a new category of actor has quietly entered most enterprise environments, and it operate...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New ‘Perseus’ Android malware checks user notes for secrets
item.description: A new Android malware called Perseus is checking user-curated notes to steal sensitive information, like passwords, recovery phrases, or financial data. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critical Microsoft SharePoint flaw now exploited in attacks
item.description: A critical Microsoft SharePoint vulnerability patched in January is now being exploited in attacks, the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) warned. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: DarkSword iOS Exploit Kit Uses 6 Flaws, 3 Zero-Days for Full Device Takeover
item.description: A new exploit kit for Apple iOS devices designed to steal sensitive data from is being wielded by multiple threat actors since at least November 2025, according to reports from Google Threat Intellige...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: EU Sanctions Companies in China, Iran for Cyberattacks
item.description: These rulings prohibit the entities from entering or doing business in the European Union....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: CISA Warns of Zimbra, SharePoint Flaw Exploits; Cisco Zero-Day Hit in Ransomware Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has urged government agencies to apply patches for two security flaws impacting Synacor Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS) and Microsoft ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Aura confirms data breach exposing 900,000 marketing contacts
item.description: Identity protection company Aura has confirmed that an unauthorized party gained access to nearly 900,000 customer records containing names and email addresses. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: C2 Implant 'SnappyClient' Targets Crypto Wallets
item.description: In addition to enabling remote access, the malware supports a wide range of capabilities, including data theft and spying....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: DarkSword: iPhone Exploit Kit Serves Spies & Thieves Alike
item.description: A sophisticated iOS exploit chain leverages multiple zero-day vulnerabilities and is targeting users in Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and Ukraine....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: CISA orders feds to patch Zimbra XSS flaw exploited in attacks
item.description: CISA has ordered U.S. government agencies to secure their servers against an actively exploited vulnerability in the Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS). [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ConnectWise patches new flaw allowing ScreenConnect hijacking
item.description: ConnectWise is warning ScreenConnect customers of a cryptographic signature verification vulnerability that could lead to unauthorized access and privilege escalation. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: OFAC Sanctions DPRK IT Worker Network Funding WMD Programs Through Fake Remote Jobs
item.description: The U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has sanctioned six individuals and two entities for their involvement in the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ransomware gang exploits Cisco flaw in zero-day attacks since January
item.description: The Interlock ransomware gang has been exploiting a maximum severity remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Cisco's Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC) software in zero-day attacks since lat...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Interlock Ransomware Exploits Cisco FMC Zero-Day CVE-2026-20131 for Root Access
item.description: Amazon Threat Intelligence is warning of an active Interlock ransomware campaign that's exploiting a recently disclosed critical security flaw in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC) Software...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Marquis: Ransomware gang stole data of 672K people in cyberattack
item.description: Marquis, a Texas-based financial services provider, revealed this week that a ransomware gang stole the data of over 670,000 individuals in an August 2025 cyberattack that also disrupted operations at...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 'Claudy Day’ Trio of Flaws Exposes Claude Users to Data Theft
item.description: A prompt injection vulnerability paired with other flaws can turn a Google search into a full attack chain that could threaten enterprise networks....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Researchers: Meta, TikTok Steal Personal & Financial Info When Users Click Ads
item.description: Tracking pixels let social media companies spy on their users even after they click over to advertiser sites, gleaning credit card info, geolocations, and more, according to an analysis....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading
